chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَبِينُ مِنْهُ الْمُطَلَّقَةُ وَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ فِيهِ الرَّجْعَةُ مَتَى يَجُوزُ لَهَا أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The time which she becomes irrevocably divorced from him, and in which there happens to be the reversion, when is it allowed for her to re-marry?

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/150)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/150)
text: الرَّجُلِ يُطَلِّقُ امْرَأَتَهُ فَقَالَ هُوَ أَحَقُّ بِرَجْعَتِهَا مَا لَمْ تَقَعْ فِي الدَّمِ الثَّالِثِ .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from Abdullah Bin Jabala, from Is’haq Bin Ammar, from Ismail Al Ju’fy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who divorces his wife, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He is more rightful with reverting back to her, for as long as the blood has not fallen regarding the third (menstruation)’.
en.sarwar: 8. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from ibn Sama‘ah from ‘Abd Allah ibn Jabalah from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar from ’Isma‘il al-Ju‘fiy who has said the following: “About the case of a man who divorces his wife, abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘He has the right to go back to her until she experiences Hayd (menses) for the third time after divorce.’”
